disposition_rationale: Original sprawling design duplicated the reflection/synthesis loops. Rescoped to the real need (mobile view + note-drop) and pressure-tested via Codex review. The rewrite narrows Slack to a thin affordance over existing substrates, with governance invariants enforced mechanically (no agent writes, tag-driven routing, idempotency spec, TRIAGE surface to preserve INBOX semantics).
evidence: Codex adversarial review (2026-04-14) identified 7 concrete issues; all addressed in the rewrite.
next_step: Close Stage 1 exit gaps (daily digest post, throttling, heartbeat/health integration), then observe the 2-week SLO window before committing to Stage 2 (inbound normalizer).
summary: Use Slack as the principal's mobile I/O surface: a notifier posts status cards for governance events, and a normalizer routes tagged inbound messages to the idea ledger, handoff INBOX, active-issues staging, or a new notes/TRIAGE surface. Scoped down from the original seven-channel intake/maintenance-agent/candidate-state-machine design after Codex review. Stage 1 (outbound) deployed 2026-04-15 against the Signal Foundry workspace.
title: Slack signal intake and friction-to-policy automation
